svg.title: Series 3 day-count rules cheat sheet
svg.desc: A Series 3 U.S. Regulations cheat sheet summarizing NFA and CFTC timing rules by deadline.

div.div: High-yield U.S. Regulations / NFA / CFTC timing rules

div.span: Watch the wording: calendar days vs. business days.

div.div: !Important: memorize whether the rule uses calendar days or business days.

tr.td: FCM generally confirms customer transactions no later than the next business day.
Daily / by noon next business day
FCM segregation, secured amount, and cleared swaps customer collateral statements are filed daily, generally by noon on the next business day.
If CPO / CTA information changed materially, the prospect must receive the Disclosure Document at least 48 hours before the subscription or advisory agreement is accepted.
A temporary AP license terminates 5 days after NFA notice that the applicant may be subject to statutory disqualification.
Certain promotional material, especially audio/video material with specific trade recommendations or profit claims, must be submitted to NFA 10 days before first use.
For security futures products, certain complaints, claims, proceedings, or disciplinary actions must be reported to NFA within 10 business days.
Voluntary promotional material pre-review should generally be submitted at least 14 calendar days before first intended use.
Certain CPO / CTA exemption or exclusion notices must be amended within 15 business days after the operator or adviser becomes aware that information is inaccurate or incomplete.
In NFA disciplinary proceedings, a respondent generally has 15 days to appeal a default decision.
Unaudited Form 1-FR financial reports are generally due within 17 business days after the statement date.
A temporary AP license can terminate if NFA has not received required items such as fingerprints, fees, or proficiency evidence within 20 days.

CPO / CTA Disclosure Documents must generally be filed with NFA at least 21 calendar days before first use; material defects or amendments generally must be corrected or filed within 21 calendar days.
AP registration is contingent on the sponsor hiring or employing the AP within 30 days.
Suspicious Activity Reports are generally due within 30 days after the firm becomes aware of suspicious activity.
CPO account statements generally must be distributed within 30 calendar days after the end of the reporting period.
A sponsor must file Form 8-T within 30 days after an AP is no longer associated.
An NFA disciplinary complaint generally must be answered within 30 days.
After a first or second Series 3 failure, the candidate generally must wait 30 days before retaking the exam.
CTA Form PR is generally due quarterly within 45 days after quarter-end or year-end.
A CPO does not need to send the final-period account statement if the Annual Report is sent within 45 calendar days after fiscal year-end.
CPO Form PQR is generally due within 60 days after calendar quarter-end.
FCM certified annual Form 1-FR and certain broker-dealer FOCUS reports are due within 60 days.
Certain CPO / CTA exemptions or exclusions must be affirmed annually within 60 days after calendar year-end.
A temporary AP transfer rule can apply when the prior AP registration terminated no more than 60 days before the new Form 8-R.
A CPO Annual Report is generally due within 90 calendar days after fiscal year-end or permanent cessation of trading.
An IB certified annual Form 1-FR is due within 90 days.
After enrolling for the Series 3, the candidate generally has 120 days to schedule and take the exam.
After a third or subsequent Series 3 failure, the candidate generally must wait 180 days before retaking the exam.
180 days = retake wait after third failure
CPO / CTA performance information in Disclosure Documents generally must be current to within 3 months.
A CPO / CTA Disclosure Document generally may not be used if it is more than 12 months old.
Series 3 proficiency generally must be satisfied within 2 years of application, unless an exception applies.
Records such as SAR support documents and promotional material generally must be retained for 5 years; promotional material is readily accessible for the first 2 years.

li.span: SAR, Form 8-T, complaint answer, AP activation, exam retake
CPO Form PQR, exemption affirmation, FCM annual certified report
CPO Annual Report, IB annual certified report

ul.li: Do not confuse calendar days with business days.
17 business days, 21 calendar days, 30 calendar days, 60 days, and 90 days are frequently tested.
Always read whether the question is about registration, disclosure, reporting, disciplinary procedure, or exam rules.

div.div: Educational exam-prep summary - verify exact wording when needed.
